title: Marketing Qualified Lead
shortDefinition: A prospect who matches your target customer profile and has shown buying intent through engagement.

content.definition: A Marketing Qualified Lead (MQL) is someone who has moved beyond raw interest and demonstrated that they fit your customer profile and have some level of buying intent. They've engaged with your marketing—downloaded content, attended a webinar, took a demo, filled out a survey—but haven't yet been vetted by sales. MQLs are the bridge between awareness and sales engagement.
content.whyItMatters: Not all leads are equal. The difference between a random email signup and someone actively exploring your pricing page is massive. By qualifying leads at the marketing stage, you avoid wasting sales time on unqualified prospects. For early-stage founders, this means your small sales team stays focused on prospects who are actually ready to buy, not random downloads. A high-quality MQL funnel multiplies your sales productivity.
content.howToApply: Define what qualifies a lead—this is critical. Typical signals: company size, job title, engagement level (opened 3+ emails, visited pricing page), content downloaded, or webinar attendance. Use lead scoring to identify MQLs automatically. Then measure the conversion rate from MQL to SQL (Sales Qualified Lead). Track which content or campaigns produce the highest-quality MQLs, not just the most MQLs. IdeaFuel's Business Plan Generator helps you define MQL criteria for your specific market and build conversion funnels that systematically move prospects from awareness to qualified leads.
commonMistakes: Equating email signups with MQLs—a signup is just the first signal. Set higher bars: engagement, profile fit, and intent signals.
Creating generic MQL criteria—'anyone who filled out a form' wastes sales time. Define specific company, title, and engagement criteria.
Not measuring MQL-to-SQL conversion—if your MQLs convert at 2% to SQL, your MQL definition is garbage. Constantly refine based on conversion data.
content.ideafuelConnection: IdeaFuel's Business Plan Generator helps you define marketing qualified lead criteria specific to your customer profile and build conversion funnels that move prospects systematically from awareness to sales engagement.

seoTitle: What is a Marketing Qualified Lead? Definition & Criteria | IdeaFuel Glossary
seoDescription: MQL is a qualified prospect matching your profile with buying intent. Learn how to identify and score MQLs effectively.
